executive_summary.one_sentence_positioning: A deep practitioner of 'vibe-coding' and AI-assisted product management, offering highly specific, battle-tested workflows for Claude Code Agent Teams and rapid MVP validation.
executive_summary.strongest_system_value: Provides exceptional, ready-to-use architectural patterns and custom plugins for Claude Code (e.g., multi-agent review pipelines, deep-thinking frameworks) backed by real-world deployment experience.
executive_summary.weakest_system_value: Lacks deep traditional software engineering fundamentals (e.g., low-level security, complex manual infrastructure scaling), relying entirely on AI to bridge technical gaps.
best_fit_queries: How to set up multi-agent workflows in Claude Code?
What are the best practices for managing token limits in AI IDEs?
How to validate product ideas rapidly using AI and AJTBD?
Claude Code vs Cursor for large projects
poor_fit_queries: How to manually optimize PostgreSQL queries for high load?
Best practices for manual Kubernetes cluster orchestration

worldview: AI has eliminated coding as a bottleneck; the new bottleneck is product decision-making.
Traditional development teams are often slower and more expensive than a single skilled 'vibe-coder'.
Rapid iteration and market validation trump perfect initial architecture.
expert_positioning.distinctive_angle: Combines Ivan Zamesin's Advanced Jobs To Be Done (AJTBD) methodology with advanced multi-agent Claude Code workflows to build and validate products at unprecedented speeds.
recurring_formats: Tool reviews (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, GLM)
Product metrics sharing (CAC, ARPU, conversion rates)

what_the_expert_knows: Intimate knowledge of Claude Code's terminal capabilities, plugin architecture, and Agent Teams.
Cost and limit management strategies across Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex.
How to bypass IDE crashes by running agents directly in terminal (e.g., iTerm).
how_they_reason: Evaluates tools based on context window management, token cost, and autonomy rather than just benchmark scores.
Prefers tools that allow deep customization (plugins/hooks) over rigid out-of-the-box solutions.

what_the_expert_knows: How to build multi-tier agent review systems (e.g., Coder -> Smoke test -> Convention check -> 3 parallel reviewers -> Tech Lead).
How to implement pre-code risk analysis using sub-agents.
How to write custom Claude Code plugins (Commands, Agents, Skills, Hooks, MCP Servers).
how_they_reason: Believes in isolating tasks to specific sub-agents to save context window and improve output quality.
Uses 'Gold Standards' (reference files) to force agents to adhere to project conventions.
missing_or_weak_subtopics: Enterprise-grade security auditing of agent outputs

what_the_expert_knows: How to apply Advanced Jobs To Be Done (AJTBD) using AI to validate ideas rapidly.
How to automate user interview analysis and extract insights/segments using AI.
How to A/B test pricing and positioning to double conversion rates.
how_they_reason: Prioritizes speed to market and user feedback over perfect initial code.
Uses AI to simulate expert debates (/expert-arena) to refine product decisions before building.

value_dimensions.rationale: High density of actionable insights, particularly regarding specific AI tool limitations, token management, and custom plugin architectures.
Provides exact GitHub repository links, step-by-step workflows, and detailed architectural breakdowns of his multi-agent systems.
Claims are backed by personal deployment experience, real-world metrics (e.g., ARPU, conversion rates), and open-source code.
Extremely valuable for teams deciding between Cursor and Claude Code, or figuring out how to structure agentic dev workflows to avoid token bankruptcy.
A primary source for 'vibe-coding' methodologies, Claude Code plugin development, and practical AI PM.
While highly enthusiastic about AI (sometimes bordering on hype), he grounds it in real limitations (e.g., token costs, IDE crashes, model degradation).

matrix_cells.notes: Exceptional depth in building custom Claude Code plugins, managing Agent Teams, and structuring multi-tier code review pipelines.
Strong comparative analysis of Cursor vs Claude Code, specifically regarding token limits, pricing, and stability under heavy agent loads.
Integrates AJTBD methodology with AI for rapid idea validation, landing page generation, and automated interview analysis.
Frequent, practical evaluations of new models (Opus 4.5/4.7, Codex 5.3, GLM 5.1, Kimi K2.6) specifically for agentic coding tasks.

query_intent_fit.why: Provides exact architectures for multi-agent teams (e.g., 4-level review pipelines, pre-code risk analysis) and custom Claude Code plugins.
limitations: Heavily biased towards Claude Code and Anthropic's ecosystem.

query_intent_fit.why: Offers deep, cost-based and stability-based comparisons between Cursor, Claude Code, Replit, and Codex.
Shares practical workflows for using AI to validate ideas, analyze competitors, and simulate expert debates before writing code.
limitations: Relies heavily on Ivan Zamesin's specific AJTBD framework.

content_quality_distribution.notes: Detailed breakdowns of custom plugins, agent architectures, and token management.
Step-by-step guides on workflows (e.g., /deep-thinking -> /feature-dev -> plan mode).
Business metrics, A/B testing results, and MVP build timelines.
Reviews of new models (Opus 4.7, GLM 5.1) and IDE updates.
General thoughts on AI replacing developers, market trends.

signature_insights.insight: Claude Code's Agent Teams allow for complex, multi-tier autonomous workflows (e.g., pre-code risk analysis, parallel reviewers, tech lead validation) that surpass single-agent IDE capabilities like Cursor.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: Provides a highly advanced, ready-to-use architectural pattern for scaling AI-assisted development beyond simple autocomplete or single-file edits.

signature_insights.insight: Running multiple AI agents simultaneously in Cursor can cause the IDE to crash; running Claude Code directly in the terminal (e.g., iTerm) resolves this and allows for massive parallelization.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: A highly practical troubleshooting tip for developers pushing the limits of AI IDEs.

signature_insights.insight: Token consumption in Claude Code scales non-linearly with context window size. To optimize costs, developers should open new chats for new tasks, use sub-agents to keep the main context clean, and avoid coding during peak US hours.
signature_insights.why_it_matters_for_experts_panel: Directly addresses the primary pain point of vibe-coding (high API/subscription costs) with actionable mitigation strategies.

practical_patterns.pattern: The '/deep-thinking -> /feature-dev -> plan mode' workflow in Claude Code.
practical_patterns.when_to_use: When starting a new feature where the exact implementation details are unclear.
failure_modes: Consumes a massive amount of tokens if not managed properly.

practical_patterns.when_to_use: Before writing code for complex features, to identify edge cases and security flaws.
failure_modes: Agents may hallucinate risks that are irrelevant to the specific tech stack.

practical_patterns.pattern: Using AI to simulate expert debates (/expert-arena) for product decisions.
practical_patterns.when_to_use: When facing a strategic or architectural dilemma with multiple valid approaches.
failure_modes: Requires high-tier models (like Opus) to generate meaningful, non-generic debates.

claims_and_positions.claim: Claude Code is superior to Cursor for complex projects due to its plugin architecture, Agent Teams, and more predictable cost structure (if managed correctly).
claims_and_positions.decision_relevance: Crucial for teams deciding which AI coding tool to adopt as their primary IDE/assistant.

claims_and_positions.claim: Russian hosting providers are blocking Telegram bot APIs, making it necessary to use foreign servers or proxies.
claims_and_positions.decision_relevance: Important infrastructure warning for developers building Telegram bots in CIS.

claims_and_positions.claim: GLM 5.1 is highly capable for agentic tasks, rivaling Sonnet 4.6 but at a fraction of the cost.
claims_and_positions.decision_relevance: Useful for developers looking to reduce API costs without sacrificing reasoning quality.

best_use_cases: Designing advanced multi-agent workflows in Claude Code.
Managing token limits and costs in AI-assisted development.
Rapidly validating product ideas using AI and AJTBD.
bad_use_cases: Deep backend infrastructure optimization without AI.
source_selection_hints: Select when the user asks about scaling AI coding beyond simple autocomplete.
Select when the user needs practical examples of Claude Code plugins.
Select when the user is struggling with high costs in Cursor or Claude.
expected_overlap_hypotheses: High overlap with Ivan Zamesin (@zamesin) on product management and AJTBD topics.
Moderate overlap with other 'vibe-coding' advocates regarding basic Cursor usage.

comment_themes: Requests for invites (Sora 2, Comet, Makers community).
Discussions on bypassing regional restrictions (VPNs, foreign cards).
Debates on the cost-effectiveness of Cursor vs Claude Code vs Codex.
Skepticism vs enthusiasm regarding AI replacing traditional developers.
questions_from_audience: How to manage token limits in Cursor/Claude?
Which VPNs work for specific AI services?
How to set up Claude Code plugins?
disagreements_or_corrections: Some community members argue that Cursor is still superior to Claude Code for certain workflows.
Debates on the ethics of exploiting 'free' educational tiers of AI services.
practical_feedback: Community members frequently share alternative tools (e.g., OpenCode, Kiro, Droid) and payment workarounds (e.g., Bybit, 5kart).
noise_or_low_value_patterns: High volume of 'give me a code/invite' comments.
Basic tech support questions (e.g., 'how to turn on VPN').

admission_implications.verdict_rationale: Ilia Izmailov provides exceptionally deep, practical, and source-backed insights into advanced AI-assisted development ('vibe-coding'). His custom plugins, multi-agent architectures, and transparent discussions on costs/limits make him a highly valuable primary source for agentic dev workflows.
unique_value_claims_to_verify_against_ma: Specific architecture of the /team-feature plugin (4-level review, pre-code risk analysis).
Cost comparison and token management strategies between Cursor and Claude Code.
likely_duplicate_areas_to_check: General AJTBD product management advice (likely duplicates Ivan Zamesin).
minimum_followup_probe_queries: Compare Claude Code Agent Teams with Cursor's multi-agent capabilities.
What are the best practices for managing token costs in AI IDEs?
risk_notes: Some workflows rely on exploiting temporary pricing loopholes or educational tiers, which may become obsolete quickly.
Heavily biased towards the Anthropic ecosystem.

unsupported_or_weak_claims: Claims about GLM 5.1 and Kimi K2.6 being superior to DeepSeek V4 are based on personal anecdotal testing rather than rigorous benchmarks.
